title: Gear Up for Adventure in the Garden Route this month
description: GEORGE NEWS - Athletes can get ready for a...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Athletes can get ready for a double dose of adrenaline-packed action as two events hit the Garden Route this month.


> First up, lace up your trail shoes for the breathtaking Oyster Catcher Trail Run along the Southern Cape coastline from 11 to 13 October.


This event is primarily designed for trail runners looking to complete their first multi-day stage race. So if you are ready to push the limits and embrace the thrill of a multi-day adventure, this is definitely for you.

A choice of two- or three-day race

The two-day race takes athletes on a 15km route between Gouritz and Boggomsbaai on day one, and a 16km route from Dana Bay to The Point in Mossel Bay on day two.

The three-day race, from 11 to 13 October, includes a 20km route on Gourikwa Nature Reserve on the first day.

For more information and to enter, please visit www.oyster-catcher.co.za or their Facebook page Oyster Catcher Trail Run.

The race follows the famous Oystercatcher hiking trail from Gouritz River Mouth to Mossel Bay via the St Blaize hiking route along the Southern Cape coastline.


> The distances covered are manageable and the terrain is mainly single track (hiking trails) and beach running, with some technical areas to add variety.


Due to the sensitivity of the area, a limited number of only 300 runners can enter each year, so don't miss out!

The distances are manageable and the terrain provides mainly single-track and beach running.


> Buckle up for Dust 4 Glory


Then, on Saturday 19 October, head to Oudtshoorn for this year's Klein Karoo Dust 4 Glory Challenge (D4G) on Saturday 19 October, at the scenic Wilgewandel Holiday Farm.

The event offers different distances and obstacles for different age and fitness groups. Participants will push their limits through mud, sweat, and grit in an epic test of endurance and can look forward to a great weekend of adventure.

D4G is now part of the Obstacle Sport Federation South Africa's (OSFSA) national series.

Event highlights

The D4G Mini is a 1 to 2km colour run course with 10 fun obstacles for kids aged 3 to 11. It is a non-timed event and an adult must accompany each child.

The D4G Grade 6 and 7 is a 3 to 4km course featuring 10 exciting obstacles. This event is perfect for school teams. D4G Medium offers a 5km route with 10 obstacles and one water point and is open to participants aged 15 and older. Expect climbing, crawling, and navigating through mud and water.

Are you up for the challenge?


> The D4G Pro is a challenging 11 to 12km course with 22 obstacles and two water points for ages 19 and older. This event tests your strength and endurance with heavy objects to carry and drag through the course.


Enjoy the beautiful surroundings and explore attractions near the venue, along with various accommodation options.

Bring your running shoes and adventurous spirit for a fantastic day. For more information or to enter, visit www.dust4glory.co.za or go to their Facebook page: Klein Karoo Dust 4 Glory Challenge.

Participants can expect to drag some heavy objects during the D4G Pro.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
